title: Add buffer time between consecutive bookings in Cal.com
feature_request: Add a buffer time setting between consecutive bookings. Back-to-back meetings are burning people out and we need breathing room between them.
phase_1_expected_questions: Should buffer time be a per-event-type setting or a global user-level setting?
Should the buffer be a fixed duration or configurable with minimum/maximum bounds?
How should buffer time display to the person booking — as blocked-out slots, or should it be invisible and just reduce available slots?
Does buffer time apply before the booking, after the booking, or both?
Should buffer time affect existing bookings or only apply to new bookings going forward?

phase_1_answers.scope: Per-event-type setting. Different event types have different needs — a 15-min check-in doesn't need the same buffer as a 60-min deep dive.
phase_1_answers.duration: Fixed duration selected from predefined options: 5, 10, 15, 30 minutes. No custom values for v1.
phase_1_answers.display: Invisible to the booker. Buffer time reduces available slots silently — the booker just sees fewer time slots. No UI indication that buffer time exists.
phase_1_answers.direction: After the booking only. Before-buffer adds complexity with overlapping calculations when multiple event types exist.
phase_1_answers.existing_bookings: Only affects new bookings. Existing bookings are not retroactively adjusted.

phase_2_expected_plan_components: Buffer time field on the event type model/schema (integer minutes, nullable)
UI control in event type settings for selecting buffer duration from predefined options
Modification to the availability/slot calculation logic to subtract buffer time after each booking
Ensure buffer time is persisted and loaded with event type configuration
API/tRPC update to accept and validate the buffer time field
Edge case: buffer time should not extend beyond the end of the user's available hours
phase_4_runtime_check: Run the TypeScript build for the UI package and the relevant scheduling logic package. Run the tests and interpret the output to verify: the buffer time selector renders with the correct predefined options, selecting a buffer value updates the event type configuration, and the slot calculation logic correctly reduces available slots by the buffer duration after existing bookings.
